authoritative_sources.title: Obtaining a Permit
authoritative_sources.publisher: New York City Department of Buildings
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NYC DOB is an authority having jurisdiction for building permits. Its public guidance shows permit filing, plan review, objections, and licensed-design-professional involvement that define clear estimating boundaries around permit approval and code interpretation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Estimate intake and permit-sensitive scope validation
used_for: refusal of permit approval and code interpretation

authoritative_sources.title: Residential Building Final Inspection Quick Sheet
authoritative_sources.publisher: Seattle Department of Construction and Inspections
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This official SDCI quick sheet provides a concrete public baseline for approved plans on site, completed associated permits, special inspection letters, and final-inspection readiness conditions that can change estimating completion posture.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Completion gating and closeout evidence review

authoritative_sources.title: Construction Industry Digest
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OSHA is the primary U.S. federal workplace safety authority. Its construction digest points to construction safety requirements and state-plan caveats that estimating must respect whenever site conditions or means assumptions change the answer.
used_for: escalation when safety changes estimate validity

authoritative_sources.title: Multi-Employer Citation Policy
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OSHA's directive is authoritative for multi-employer worksite responsibilities. It clarifies creating, exposing, correcting, and controlling employer roles that affect estimating assumptions tied to sequencing, subcontractors, and changed site safety conditions.

authoritative_sources.title: Cost Estimating
authoritative_sources.source_class: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: WBDG is a recognized public building guidance platform used by federal and industry practitioners. Its cost-estimating guidance defines estimate methods, estimate purpose, documentation expectations, and estimating vocabulary relevant to construction scope and quantity support.

authoritative_sources.title: Develop and Manage Costs for RWA Projects
authoritative_sources.source_class: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GSA is an authoritative public construction owner. Its cost-management guidance distinguishes estimate types and maturity across project delivery, which informs lifecycle-state progression and what evidence is required before an estimate can be treated as ready or complete.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Estimate lifecycle progression and review readiness

authoritative_sources.title: EP 415-1-260 Area/Resident Engineer Management Guide
authoritative_sources.source_class: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: USACE is an authoritative public construction owner. Its management guide gives explicit operational guidance for change pricing, two-part change orders, contingency, and estimate development under uncertain design maturity, which directly constrains estimating exception handling.

authoritative_sources.title: Professional Guidance Documents
authoritative_sources.source_class: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AACE International is a recognized professional authority for cost engineering and estimate classification. Its public guidance catalog anchors estimate classification and basis terminology used to keep the lane specific to estimating practice.

workflow_stages.name: Estimate intake and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Classify the request, confirm jurisdiction, site, permit, work-order, drawing revision, and tenant approval context, and decide whether estimating can start safely.
source_classes: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Estimate basis assembly and in-flight execution
workflow_stages.description: Normalize scope, takeoff, quote coverage, assumptions, exclusions, schedule constraints, and estimate class into an in-progress estimating package with explicit evidence traceability.
source_classes: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance

workflow_stages.name: Exception, rework, and changed-condition handling
workflow_stages.description: Reopen or hold estimate work when site conditions, safety findings, permit-set drift, subcontractor quote gaps, or immature change design make deterministic pricing unsafe.
source_classes: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Review readiness, completion evidence, and closure
workflow_stages.description: Move estimate work to ready-for-review, issued, or closed only when the basis, supporting quotes, review records, and completion or closeout dependencies are explicit and authoritative.
source_classes: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards

artifacts: Drawings, specifications, addenda, and basis-of-design notes
Subcontractor and vendor quotes and bid tabs
Assumptions, exclusions, alternates, and allowance logs
Permit records, approved plans, and inspection records
Site walk notes, photos, site logs, and safety observations
Change orders, RFIs, design clarifications, and review logs
Closeout packages, correction notices, and completion evidence

allowed: Assess whether estimate inputs, permit context, site facts, and source-traceable pricing support a bounded estimating output.
Advance, hold, rework, issue, or close estimate work based on documented lifecycle state and evidence completeness.
Prepare estimate packages, status summaries, exception notes, and escalation packets without claiming design, permit, trade, or financial approval authority.
escalate: Permit, inspection, design, or safety conditions that materially change estimate assumptions or completion posture
Conflicting tenant policy and public baseline on estimate closure, permit handling, or approval sequencing
Requests that move into project controls, procurement award, finance approval, superintendent, design, permitting, inspection, or licensed trade ownership
refuse: Permit approval, code interpretation, engineer-of-record judgment, or licensed trade signoff
Final contract award, budget approval, contingency authorization, or final change-order approval
Any request to mark estimate work complete without required basis, quote, review, permit, inspection, or completion evidence

domain_failure_modes: Pricing work from stale drawings, unresolved permit assumptions, or missing approved-plan context.
Treating provisional site assumptions or subcontractor placeholders as confirmed pricing without explicit uncertainty handling.
Closing estimate work while takeoff backup, quote support, review logs, or closeout evidence are incomplete.
Allowing schedule pressure to suppress changed safety conditions, inspection blockers, or permit-sensitive scope changes.
Acting as code interpreter, licensed trade authority, engineer of record, or final commercial approver instead of escalating.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Request that implies licensed trade or code-interpretation authority
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Public sources show common permit and final-inspection patterns, but exact approved-plan posting, associated permit, and inspection sequencing rules remain jurisdiction-specific. The pack therefore requires tenant jurisdiction context and escalates when it is missing.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific estimate approval thresholds and markup policies
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Public estimating guidance does not supply tenant approval chains, markup limits, or contingency authorization rules. The pack treats those as tenant-required facts and refuses final approval behavior without them.
